title: Pressure-Cooked French Parmigiano-Reggiano Bake
description: A rich and creamy French-inspired bake with a floral twist, featuring Parmigiano-Reggiano and a gluten-free friendly crust in a clay pot.
instructions: Preheat a pressure cooker to high pressure.
Rub the inside of a clay pot with butter and coat with gluten-free flour.
In a separate bowl, mix together Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese, thyme, fleur de sel, and a pinch of black pepper.
Place the clay pot in the pressure cooker and add the garlic and rosemary to the pot.
Pour the heavy cream into the pressure cooker and close the lid.
Set the pressure cooker to high pressure for 10 minutes.
After the cooking time has elapsed, allow the pressure to release naturally for 5 minutes.
Remove the clay pot from the pressure cooker and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for an additional 10 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
Carve into slices and serve immediately.
